summary: Dan Shapiro announces the public launch of Sparkbuy, a product he had been working on for six months. The post is a brief announcement directing readers to the site and press coverage from the New York Times, Wall Street Journal's All Things D, and GeekWire. Sparkbuy was positioned as a 'Kayak for gadgets' — a comparison search engine for consumer electronics. The post is essentially a launch day placeholder with links rather than a substantive discussion.
key_points: Sparkbuy launched publicly as a comparison search engine for consumer electronics like laptops and TVs
The product was described in press as 'a Kayak for gadgets', positioning it as an aggregator in the electronics shopping space
The launch garnered coverage from major outlets including the New York Times and Wall Street Journal
money_quotes: Check it out at www.sparkbuy.com and see what I've been working on for the past 6 months or so.
key_insight: Shapiro launched Sparkbuy as a comparison search engine for consumer electronics, attracting major press coverage by positioning it as 'a Kayak for gadgets.'
